topic: Hanshin’s 24-year-old right-hander Taisei Kudo turns a record-setting 163 km/h fastball and a bases-loaded escape into his first professional win.
facts:
VERIFIED FACTS (confirmed by Nikkan Sports, NPB - use as-is, do not invent numbers/quotes beyond these):
- On July 11, 2026, Hanshin defeated Yakult 2–1 at Hanshin Koshien Stadium.
- Kudo entered in the eighth inning and faced the bases-loaded, no-out situation after a hit, an error, and a walk.
- Kudo recorded a top speed of 163 km/h, surpassing his previous personal best of 161 km/h.
- The 163 km/h pitch matched Roberto Suárez’s Hanshin club record.
- It became the fastest pitch reported for a Japanese pitcher in Hanshin history, exceeding Shintaro Fujinami’s previous 162 km/h mark.
- Kudo helped record the first out at home on a ground ball, then struck out Leandro Cedeño and Domingo Santana.
- Kudo threw 24 pitches in the inning, including nine above 160 km/h.
- On July 12, Hanshin beat Yakult 3–0, and Kudo received his first professional win.
- Hanshin entered July 13 in first place in the Central League with a 43–35–1 record according to the NPB team page.
- Kudo was born in 2001, joined Hanshin as a developmental draft player in 2024, and was promoted to the official roster before the 2025 season.

CONTESTED / SINGLE-SOURCE (attribute clearly, do not present as settled fact):
- Full-Count reported that a DAZN social-media video featuring Kudo’s pitch received about 25,000 likes in half a day; this engagement figure is not independently confirmed here.
- Full-Count summarized online reactions describing Kudo’s delivery as slightly quick and the moment as emotionally overwhelming; these reactions do not represent an independently verified survey.
- Reports differ in how they describe the 163 km/h mark: some call it a tie for Hanshin’s overall club record, while others emphasize it as the new Japanese-player record for the club.

GENERAL KNOWLEDGE (you may explain these established concepts from your own knowledge):
- A developmental player in Japanese baseball begins outside the main 70-player roster and must earn promotion, making Kudo’s rise an underdog story.
- Koshien is Hanshin’s home stadium and is known for its intense, highly emotional fan atmosphere.
- A 160 km/h pitch is exceptional in professional baseball, and 163 km/h is elite-level velocity even by NPB standards.
- Relief pitchers often enter in high-pressure situations, so Kudo’s achievement involved protecting a one-run lead with the bases loaded rather than simply recording a radar-gun highlight.

SPECULATION (encouraged, but always clearly framed, e.g. "If X happens..."):
- If Kudo can repeat this level of velocity and command, he could become a dependable part of Hanshin’s bullpen during the demanding summer schedule.
- If his first professional win leads to continued opportunities, the 163 km/h escape may become the defining starting point of his breakout season.
headlines: Nikkan Sports: Taisei Kudo reaches a personal-best 163 km/h while escaping a bases-loaded crisis with repeated 160 km/h pitches.
Sanspo Kansai: Catcher Ryutaro Umeno praises Kudo’s emotionally charged 163 km/h fastball.
Full-Count: A former developmental player’s astonishing 163 km/h pitch sparks a major reaction from fans.
Daily Sports: Kudo surpasses Shintaro Fujinami’s Japanese-player mark for Hanshin and matches Roberto Suárez’s club record.
NPB: Official game report records Hanshin’s 2–1 victory over Yakult at Koshien on July 11, 2026.
Nikkan Sports: Kudo earns his first professional win one day after his 163 km/h breakthrough.
article_summaries:
Lead sentences by outlet (paraphrased close to the original opening line, not verbatim):

Nikkan Sports: The report opens with Kudo’s personal-best 163 km/h pitch and his dramatic eighth-inning escape from a bases-loaded, no-out situation at Koshien.

Sanspo Kansai: The article begins with Umeno’s praise for Kudo and places the 163 km/h pitch within Hanshin’s 2–1 win over Yakult.

Full-Count: The opening presents Kudo as a product of Hanshin’s developmental-player system and says his 163 km/h pitch caused the stadium to erupt and fans online to react strongly.

Daily Sports: The report starts with 42,641 spectators watching the stadium speed display as Kudo overcame trouble he had helped create.

NPB: The official page records the completed July 11 Central League game at Koshien, with Hanshin defeating Yakult 2–1.

Nikkan Sports: The follow-up report presents Kudo’s first professional win as the immediate sequel to his 163 km/h breakthrough and connects it to Hanshin maintaining first place.
